seoTitle: How to Add Any DNS Record Type in Cloudflare
metaDescription: Build A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, and TXT records in Cloudflare from a single workflow, covering priorities, proxy status, and TTL settings.
intro: Once you know the basic workflow for adding a record in Cloudflare, every record type follows the same pattern with a few type-specific fields. This guide walks through the full add-record process, covering the most common record types you'll need.

steps.heading: Open the DNS Records grid
steps.body: Select your target domain and open the DNS Records page from the sidebar.

steps.heading: Click Add Record
steps.body: Click the '+ Add Record' button to launch the configuration panel.

steps.heading: Configure an A record
steps.body: Set your root handle, input your hosting IP address, and save.

steps.heading: Add CNAME aliases
steps.body: Create subdomain CNAME records and toggle between proxied and DNS Only modes as needed.

steps.heading: Set up MX and TXT rows
steps.body: Add mail MX records with priority values, paste in TXT verification strings, adjust TTL settings, and review your completed records table for accuracy.

faq.question: Do all record types need different proxy settings?
faq.answer: Web-facing records like A and CNAME can typically be proxied, while mail-related records like MX and most TXT records should stay on DNS Only.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to check my records are all set up correctly?
faq.answer: Review the completed records table in the DNS panel, or run an external propagation check to confirm everything resolves as expected.

faq.question: Can I add multiple record types for the same subdomain?
faq.answer: Generally you'll use one primary record type per host name (like an A or CNAME), though you can layer TXT records alongside them for verification purposes.

faq.question: What happens if I set the wrong priority on an MX record?
faq.answer: An incorrect priority can cause mail to route to the wrong server first, so double-check the numeric value against your mail provider's documentation.
